task: Add social login to existing React Native + TypeScript app
role: You are an expert React Native + TypeScript developer.
description: Add social login (Google, Facebook, X/Twitter, Microsoft/Outlook) to an existing app that already has working email/password login and registration.

step_1_trace_first.instruction: Before writing ANY code, read and fully understand these files
extract_from_files: Existing component structure, styling patterns, UI conventions
How the login API call is made (endpoint, request shape, response shape)
How JWT/token is saved to SecureStore after login
How navigation works after successful login
TypeScript types/interfaces already defined for auth (User, AuthResponse, etc.)
Existing AuthContext, Redux slice, or Zustand store managing auth state
also_trace_imports: API client being used (axios instance / fetch wrapper)
Shared UI components (Button, Input, Divider, etc.) already in the codebase
step_1_trace_first.rule: Do NOT invent new patterns. Mirror exactly what already exists.

after_success: Save JWT to SecureStore using the SAME function already used in useAuthLogin.ts
Update auth state using the SAME context/store pattern already in the app
Navigate using navigation.replace() to the same screen existing login navigates to

rules: Mirror structure of useAuthLogin.ts exactly — same loading state, error state, return shape
Export: { loginWithGoogle, loginWithFacebook, loginWithTwitter, loginWithOutlook, isLoading, error }
Each function: triggers provider SDK → gets token → calls POST /auth/social → saves JWT → updates auth state
On failure: set typed error message

props.onLoginSuccess: (user: User) => void — used in login mode only
props.onRegisterRedirect: () => void — used in register mode only

rules: Renders divider 'or continue with' + 4 provider buttons
Each button shows provider icon (react-native-svg) and provider name
Show loading indicator on active button only during login
Show error in the same style existing errors are shown in the screen
Both LoginScreen and RegisterScreen import this component
Keep ALL existing code exactly as-is
Add <SocialLoginButtons mode='login' onLoginSuccess={...} /> below existing login form
Call useSocialAuth hook the same pattern as useAuthLogin is called today
On social login success: save token + navigate (same as email login flow)
Keep ALL existing code exactly as-is
Add <SocialLoginButtons mode='register' onRegisterRedirect={...} /> below existing register form
Social buttons on register screen do NOT save a token and do NOT call POST /auth/social
On social provider SDK success: navigate to LoginScreen
Pass provider and email as navigation params to LoginScreen

typescript_rules: Reuse existing User and AuthResponse types — do not redefine them
Extend existing types if needed, never duplicate
hard_constraints: Do NOT change existing email login or registration flow
Do NOT add a new token storage utility — reuse what exists in the codebase
Do NOT add a new AuthContext — plug into whatever already exists
Mirror file naming conventions found in the codebase
Mirror import style (absolute vs relative paths) already used in the project
output_rules: Full working code — no placeholders except TODO for provider credentials
Add TODO comments wherever a client ID or redirect URI must be configured
Do not truncate — output the complete file every time
final_verification_checklist: Token is saved ONLY in login flow — never in register flow
Register social flow ends with navigation to LoginScreen
Login social flow ends with navigation.replace() to home screen
No existing email login or registration functionality is broken
All new code passes strict TypeScript — no implicit any, no ts-ignore
